scope_description: Independent review of the shell/agent create flow (backend web-create-endpoints + frontend web-create-sheet Phase 1+2) and home-shell-workspaces (shell-only workspaces surfaced on the home screen), per explicit user request to review both features now. Scoped at cell granularity so the verification preflight actually inspects each behavior-change cell's evidence.

included.reason: optional cwd on tab_create/agent_start, fake growth
wire FAB and sheet into switcher
render shell-only workspaces on home (absorbed cell 3's scope)

excluded.reason: parent enabling feature predating this batch; not named by the user, reviewed separately if requested
dropped during validation; scope merged into home-shell-workspaces-2 to resolve a whole-project-typecheck deadlock

head: 64493ce0871eb4cce4335af270a5254541e416ed

reviewers.focus: auth, secrets, injection, permissions, data exposure
missing edge cases, regression paths, weak assertions
new/changed routes and public response shapes: GET /api/create-options, POST /api/panes, POST /api/agents, GET /api/agents {agents,shells} shape change

findings.summary: docs/specs/web-api.md documents Destination.path/ShellRow.path as absent on a join miss; the wire actually sends the key with a null value.
findings.today: Destination.path and ShellRow.path are Option<String> with no skip_serializing_if, so a join miss serializes {"path": null} (key present). TypeScript agrees: path: string | null.
findings.why_it_matters: docs/specs/web-api.md Data Dictionary items 4 and 11 say Default: absent. Absent-key and present-but-null are different JSON contracts, and the spec is the declared source of truth for this surface.
findings.failure_scenario: A future non-JS integrator coding from the spec tests "path" in obj or treats the field as non-nullable and breaks on the actual null.
findings.fix: Update the spec's two Data Dictionary rows from "absent" to "null when the join misses" (code/frontend/tests already agree on null; changing them to omit the key is higher risk).

findings.summary: The only test proving P10's agent-refuse half (createroute_agent_unresolved_path_is_refused_409) would still pass if the anchor-refuse guard were deleted, so it does not actually protect the locked decision.
findings.today: The test posts workspace_id="ghost-workspace" (a nonexistent workspace) and asserts 409. A nonexistent workspace also 409s through the port itself (FakeHerdr::agent_start returns agent_placement_not_found), so the 409 is over-determined and the guard's own effect is never isolated.
findings.why_it_matters: P10 is the security-relevant decision that an agent must never start in herdr's arbitrary process directory. The shell half IS correctly tested against a workspace that exists but whose anchor fails to resolve (RecordingHerdr, asserting tab_cwd == Some(None)); the agent half has no equivalent test.
findings.failure_scenario: Remove the anchor-refuse guard in src/web/create.rs. Against an existing-but-unresolvable workspace with RecordingHerdr, agent_start would return Ok/200 and record agent_cwd == Some(None) -- a real regression -- while the existing ghost-workspace test stays green and misses it.
findings.fix: Add a mirror test using stub_state(unresolvable("wx")) + RecordingHerdr for POST /api/agents: assert 409 AND that herdr.agent_cwd stays None (port never called), matching the shell-side test's shape.

findings.summary: buildHomeGroups' correctness silently depends on a backend invariant (shells and agents never share a workspace) that is enforced only by the backend filter and prose comments across two files/languages, with no test pinning the contract.
findings.today: web/src/views/switcher.ts:82-88 asserts in a comment that the two arrays never share a workspace_id; the only enforcement is the backend filter at src/web/api.rs:66 (!agent_workspaces.contains(...)). No test exercises what happens if a ShellRow's workspace_id ever matched an existing agent group.
findings.why_it_matters: If that backend invariant is ever relaxed (e.g. to surface shell panes alongside an agent in the same workspace), buildHomeGroups would render that workspace twice on the home screen -- silently, with no type error and no failing test to catch it.
findings.failure_scenario: A future change to the shell filter in src/web/api.rs (intentional or not) that lets a shell row's workspace_id equal an existing agent group's id -- home screen shows a duplicate group entry.
findings.fix: Either make buildHomeGroups merge a shell row into an existing agent group by workspace_id instead of assuming disjointness, or add a test that pins the disjoint-groups contract so a future relaxation of the backend filter is caught.

findings.summary: collisionSuffixes' 4-character workspace_id suffix can itself collide, leaving two colliding {label,path} destinations still indistinguishable in the create sheet -- flagged independently by two reviewers (code-quality and test-coverage), promoted from P3 to P2 on corroboration.
findings.today: web/src/views/create-sheet.ts's collisionSuffixes appends dest.workspace_id.slice(-4) to disambiguate destinations that already collide on label+path. Two colliding destinations whose ids share the same last 4 characters render an identical suffix and remain ambiguous. The existing test only exercises ids with distinct tails.
findings.why_it_matters: The whole point of S1 disambiguation is to let the operator tell two colliding destinations apart before creating a pane/agent in the wrong one; a suffix collision defeats that purpose silently.
findings.failure_scenario: Two workspaces with the same label+path whose ids end in the same 4 characters (e.g. short or structured herdr ids) -- the create sheet shows two rows with identical visible text, operator cannot tell them apart.
findings.evidence: web/src/views/create-sheet.ts collisionSuffixes function and its test in web/test/create-sheet.test.ts (only distinct-tail ids exercised)
findings.fix: Fall back to the full workspace_id (or a longer slice) when the 4-character suffixes still collide; add a test pinning the fallback.

uat.item: Formal SEE/CALL/RUN walkthrough of all three features' locked decisions
uat.reason: User already hands-on confirmed the shipped behavior multiple times during the build session (e.g. confirming the home-screen shell card, confirming PBI-024 closure) rather than in a separate formal walkthrough; user explicitly chose to skip a fresh walkthrough at Gate 4.

gate4.note: User explicitly approved merge after reviewing the synthesis (0 P1, 4 P2, 8 P3, all filed to backlog) and explicitly chose to skip a formal UAT walkthrough.
